metadata.archetype_justification: Primary workload pattern is event-driven document processing — Lambda functions triggered by DynamoDB Streams, SQS queues, and S3 notifications. The API surface (API Gateway) exists but is secondary to the core event-processing pipeline.

top_gaps.title: Deployment Strategy
top_gaps.gap: Direct-to-production CDK deploy with no staged rollout
top_gaps.impact: High risk of production incidents with no ability to canary or rollback

top_gaps.title: Integration Testing
top_gaps.gap: Only 2 basic unit test files; no integration or end-to-end tests in CI

top_gaps.title: Backup and Recovery
top_gaps.gap: No backup configuration for DynamoDB (no PITR) or Elasticsearch; S3 versioning disabled
top_gaps.impact: Data loss risk with no recovery path

top_gaps.title: CI/CD Automation
top_gaps.gap: Single-stage CodePipeline with minimal test coverage

top_gaps.title: Secrets Management
top_gaps.gap: No Secrets Manager or Vault; config via environment variables with no rotation
top_gaps.impact: No credential rotation or audit trail

findings.title: Managed Databases — single-AZ Elasticsearch in non-CICD path
findings.description: DynamoDB and Amazon Elasticsearch Service are both managed, but the non-CICD deployment path deploys Elasticsearch as a single-instance cluster without zone awareness.
findings.gap: Elasticsearch deployed single-AZ in non-CICD mode. Legacy Amazon Elasticsearch Service naming.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Amazon OpenSearch Service. Ensure all deployment paths use zone-awareness with ≥2 AZs.

findings.title: Workflow Orchestration — ad hoc pipeline in handler code
findings.description: Document processing pipeline implemented as Lambda-to-SQS routing without dedicated orchestration. The documentProcessor Lambda manually routes by file type. Error handling relies on timeout-based DLQ detection.
findings.gap: No Step Functions or equivalent orchestrating the multi-step document processing workflow.
findings.recommendation: Adopt AWS Step Functions for the document processing pipeline to gain visual monitoring, built-in retry/error handling, and parallel processing control.

findings.title: Network Security — overly permissive security group
findings.description: VPC with private subnets configured. Security group allows ALL traffic from ANY IPv4 (Peer.anyIpv4(), Port.allTraffic()).
findings.gap: Security group allows 0.0.0.0/0 on all ports within the VPC.
findings.recommendation: Replace Peer.anyIpv4() with VPC CIDR or self-referencing security group rule.

findings.title: API Entry Point — no throttling configured
findings.description: API Gateway with Cognito auth and request validation, but no usage plan or throttling. WAF not integrated.
findings.gap: No throttling or rate limiting on API Gateway.
findings.recommendation: Add API Gateway usage plan with rate limiting and burst throttling. Consider WAF.

findings.title: Auto-Scaling — Elasticsearch has no auto-scaling
findings.description: Lambda scales automatically. DynamoDB uses PAY_PER_REQUEST. Elasticsearch has fixed instance type with no auto-scaling.
findings.gap: Elasticsearch has no auto-scaling. Lambda reserved concurrency cap (30) may be insufficient.
findings.recommendation: Enable auto-scaling for OpenSearch. Review Lambda concurrency limits.

findings.title: Backup and Recovery — no backup configuration
findings.description: No backup configuration for any data store. DynamoDB lacks PITR. S3 versioning disabled. No Elasticsearch snapshots. No AWS Backup plans.
findings.gap: Complete absence of backup and recovery configuration.
findings.recommendation: Enable DynamoDB PITR, S3 versioning on documents bucket, automated ES snapshots, and AWS Backup plan.

findings.title: High Availability — single-AZ Elasticsearch in non-CICD path
findings.description: Lambda and DynamoDB are inherently multi-AZ. CICD path has multi-AZ ES. Non-CICD path has single-instance ES.
findings.gap: Non-CICD deployment has single-AZ Elasticsearch.
findings.recommendation: Ensure all deployment paths use multi-AZ Elasticsearch/OpenSearch.

findings.title: IaC Coverage — no operational resources in IaC
findings.description: Core infrastructure comprehensively defined in CDK. CICD pipeline in CloudFormation. But no CloudWatch alarms, dashboards, or backup plans in IaC.
findings.gap: No operational/DR resources (alarms, dashboards, backup plans) in IaC.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch alarms, dashboards, and AWS Backup plans as CDK constructs.

findings.title: CI/CD Automation — single-stage pipeline
findings.description: CloudFormation-based CodePipeline with Source→Deploy. Build, test, and deploy combined in one CodeBuild step. No quality gates.
findings.gap: No separate test stage. No IaC validation. No multi-environment promotion.
findings.recommendation: Restructure: Source → Build/Test → Staging → Integration Test → Production. Add cdk-nag validation.

findings.title: Programming Languages — compound legacy signals
findings.description: Backend uses Python 3.8 (EOL), Java 8 (EOL), CDK v1 (EOL), boto3 1.13.20 (severely outdated), aws-sdk v2. Frontend is modern (Next.js 14.1, React 18).
findings.gap: Compound version debt: Python 3.8 + Java 8 + CDK v1 + boto3 1.13.20 + aws-sdk v2.
findings.recommendation: Migrate CDK v1→v2, Python 3.8→3.12+, update boto3, aws-sdk v2→v3, Java 8→21.

findings.title: Monolith vs Microservices — modular but single-stack
findings.description: 12+ Lambda functions with distinct responsibilities communicating via SQS/SNS/DynamoDB Streams. Modular serverless architecture but deployed as a single CDK stack with shared DynamoDB tables.
findings.gap: Single CDK stack deployment. Shared DynamoDB tables. No independent deployment of functions.
findings.recommendation: Consider splitting into independent stacks: API stack, processing pipeline stack, shared resources stack.

findings.title: API Versioning Strategy — no versioning
findings.description: API endpoints are unversioned: /search, /documents, /document, /redact. No /v1/ prefix, no version headers.
findings.gap: No API versioning strategy. Breaking changes affect all consumers simultaneously.
findings.recommendation: Implement URL-path versioning (e.g., /v1/documents). Define backward-compatibility policy.

findings.title: Service Discovery — static environment variables
findings.description: All service references passed as environment variables at deploy time. No dynamic discovery.
findings.gap: Static configuration — no runtime service discovery.
findings.recommendation: For serverless, environment variables are acceptable. Consider SSM Parameter Store for shared configuration.

findings.title: Unified Data Access Layer — ES access scattered
findings.description: Centralized DocumentStore class for DynamoDB. Elasticsearch access scattered across multiple Lambda functions without abstraction.
findings.gap: No centralized Elasticsearch access layer.
findings.recommendation: Create centralized OpenSearch access layer in the helper Lambda layer.

findings.title: Database Engine Version and EOL — Elasticsearch 7.4 legacy
findings.description: Elasticsearch 7.4 pinned in CDK. Amazon Elasticsearch Service is legacy naming (now OpenSearch Service). Engine version is outdated.
findings.gap: Elasticsearch 7.4 on deprecated service. Migration to OpenSearch needed.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Amazon OpenSearch Service 2.x. Update CDK construct.

findings.title: Encryption at Rest — mixed encryption types
findings.description: All data stores encrypted. S3 uses SSE-S3, DynamoDB uses AWS-owned key, SQS and ES use customer-managed KMS with key rotation.
findings.gap: S3 and DynamoDB use AWS-managed/owned encryption instead of customer-managed KMS.
findings.recommendation: Migrate sensitive S3 buckets to SSE-KMS. Enable customer-managed CMK for DynamoDB.

findings.title: Centralized Identity — app-specific Cognito pool
findings.description: Application-specific Cognito User Pool with admin-create-only users. No federation with organizational IdP. No SSO.
findings.gap: No integration with centralized organizational identity provider.
findings.recommendation: Configure Cognito federation with organizational IdP (SAML/OIDC). Enable SSO.

findings.title: Secrets Management — env vars without rotation
findings.description: No plaintext credentials. Config via CDK environment variables. IAM-based auth for DynamoDB/ES. No Secrets Manager or Vault.
findings.gap: No secrets management service. No rotation capability.
findings.recommendation: Adopt Secrets Manager for future credentials. Use SSM Parameter Store (SecureString) for sensitive config.

findings.title: Compute Hardening — deprecated runtimes
findings.description: Lambda runtime patching is managed by AWS. However Python 3.8 and Java 8 runtimes are past EOL. No vulnerability scanning.
findings.gap: Deprecated runtimes no longer receiving patches. No vulnerability scanning.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade runtimes to Python 3.12+ and Java 21. Enable AWS Inspector. Add dependency scanning.

findings.title: Application Security Pipeline — no security scanning
findings.description: No SAST, no dependency scanning, no security gates in pipeline. No Dependabot, no npm audit, no pip-audit.
findings.gap: Complete absence of automated security validation.
findings.recommendation: Add cdk-nag, npm audit, pip-audit, Dependabot, and Semgrep. Gate deployments on critical findings.

findings.title: SLO Definitions — no SLOs defined
findings.description: No SLO definitions. No CloudWatch alarms. No error budget tracking.
findings.gap: No formal service level objectives.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for API response time, document processing time, and availability.

findings.title: Business Metrics — no custom metrics
findings.description: No custom business metrics published. Only print() logging. No CloudWatch put_metric_data.
findings.gap: No visibility into business outcomes.
findings.recommendation: Publish custom metrics: documents_processed, processing_duration, processing_errors, search_queries.

findings.title: Anomaly Detection and Alerting — no alerting
findings.description: No CloudWatch alarms. No anomaly detection. DLQ messages accumulate silently.
findings.gap: Failures are completely silent. No alerting on any metric.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch alarms for Lambda errors, DLQ depth, API 5xx, ES cluster health. Enable anomaly detection.

findings.title: Deployment Strategy — direct to production
findings.description: CDK deploy directly updates production CloudFormation stack. No canary, blue/green, or traffic shifting.
findings.gap: All changes go directly to production with no staged validation or automatic rollback.
findings.recommendation: Implement Lambda deployment preferences with CodeDeploy canary. Add automatic rollback on alarm breach.

findings.title: Integration Testing — minimal test coverage
findings.description: Only 2 Python unit test files. No integration tests for the document processing workflow. No API tests.
findings.gap: No integration test coverage for critical document processing pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Create integration test suite covering upload→process→retrieve workflow. Run in CI against staging.

findings.title: Incident Response Automation — no runbooks
findings.description: No runbooks, no SSM Automation, no self-healing. DLQs capture failures but no automated remediation.
findings.gap: Incident response is entirely ad hoc.
findings.recommendation: Create DLQ redrive automation. SSM runbooks for common incidents. Self-healing on known failure modes.

findings.title: Observability Ownership — no dashboards or owners
findings.description: No dashboards in IaC. No CODEOWNERS. No named alarm owners. X-Ray traces exist but no dashboards.
findings.gap: No observability ownership model.
findings.recommendation: Create CloudWatch dashboard in CDK. Add CODEOWNERS. Define alarm ownership.

findings.title: Resource Tagging Governance — no tags
findings.description: No tags on any resources. No tagging strategy. No cost allocation tags.
findings.gap: All resources are untagged. No cost tracking or ownership identification.
findings.recommendation: Add mandatory tags (Environment, Service, Owner, CostCenter) via CDK Tags.of(). Enable cost allocation tags.

evaluations.title: Managed Compute
evaluations.reason: All compute on Lambda (serverless). No EC2 for application workloads.

evaluations.title: Async Messaging and Streaming
evaluations.reason: All messaging on managed services (SNS, SQS, DynamoDB Streams, S3 notifications) with structured consumer patterns.

evaluations.title: Async vs Sync Communication
evaluations.reason: Event-processor archetype: primary input is async (DynamoDB Streams, SQS). Outbound calls appropriately use sync/async Textract APIs.

evaluations.title: Long-Running Process Handling
evaluations.reason: Event-processor archetype: handlers are async by design. PDFs routed to async Textract with SNS completion callbacks.

evaluations.title: Unstructured Data Storage
evaluations.reason: Documents in S3 with Textract, Comprehend, and Kendra parsing pipeline.

evaluations.title: Stored Procedures and Schema Complexity
evaluations.reason: No stored procedures. All business logic in Lambda. DynamoDB is schemaless.

evaluations.title: Audit Logging
evaluations.reason: Application-level IaC only. CloudTrail is an account-level concern belonging in foundation infrastructure repo.

evaluations.title: API Authentication
evaluations.reason: All API endpoints authenticated via Cognito User Pool authorizer.

evaluations.title: Distributed Tracing
evaluations.reason: All Lambda functions have X-Ray active tracing. Automatic propagation through SQS/SNS/DynamoDB/API Gateway.

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2 = 3 — application already has modular serverless architecture with independently deployable Lambda functions.

triggering_questions.note: APP-Q2 = 3 meets threshold; pathway not needed

pathways.not_triggered_reason: APP-Q2 >= 3: application is already a modular serverless architecture.

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1 = 4 — compute already on Lambda (serverless). Container pathway does not apply.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: Workload already runs on Lambda (serverless); container pathway does not apply.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4 = 4 — no stored procedures or proprietary SQL. No commercial database engines detected.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2 = 3 — databases already use managed services (DynamoDB, Amazon Elasticsearch Service).

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q2 = 3 meets threshold; databases are managed

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4 = 4 — messaging already on managed services. No data processing workloads requiring analytics migration.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q11 = 2 (primary trigger met); OPS-Q5 = 1, OPS-Q6 = 1 (supporting conditions strengthen)

detail.current_state: Single-stage CodePipeline with inline buildspec. Direct-to-production CDK deploy. Only 2 unit test files.
immediate_actions: Restructure pipeline with separate test stage
Add Lambda deployment preferences with canary

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. The existing Textract/Comprehend/Kendra usage is document understanding (not generative AI/LLM/agent patterns).
